product.name: Daily Pulse
product.tagline: One tap to log your whole day
product.one_liner: a fast, offline, installable life-tracker — mood, energy, habits, workouts, journal and tasks in one tap, private on your device with optional Google Sheet sync

features.name: Habit Tracker
features.does: a 10-habit daily checklist with per-habit streaks and a 90-day consistency heatmap
features.proof: streaks 🔥 and heatmaps that make a missed day obvious the next morning

features.name: Mood Tracker
features.does: a one-tap mood and energy log with trend charts over time
features.proof: mood and energy trend lines you can actually read after a few weeks

features.name: Workout Log
features.does: an 8-group, 42-exercise gym plan with animated how-to demos, reps/weight logging and a gym streak
features.proof: a real Fitness-Zone plan with animated form demos, not a blank spreadsheet

features.name: Daily Journal
features.does: a went-well / to-improve / free-journal block plus a deep log organised into Mind, Wellbeing, Health, Work, Learning, Finance, Digital and Growth
features.proof: structured prompts so the page is never blank, plus a free space when you need it

features.name: Task Manager
features.does: quick task add, check-off, and automatic carry-over flagging for anything you didn't finish
features.proof: carried-over tasks get flagged so nothing quietly disappears

features.name: Sleep Tracker
features.does: a daily sleep-hours log charted against your deep-work and energy
features.proof: sleep plotted next to focus and energy, so the connection is visible

features.name: Focus Tracker
features.does: a daily deep-work / focus-hours log with a trend chart
features.proof: deep-work hours trended over 30 days, not guessed at

features.name: Weekly Review
features.does: an automatic Sunday weekly-review block that folds into your normal log
features.proof: the review appears on its own every Sunday — no separate ritual to remember

audiences.name: Students
audiences.pain: juggling classes, assignments and revision while trying to keep any kind of routine
audiences.hook: between lectures and deadlines, a tracker only works if it takes seconds

audiences.name: Software Developers
audiences.pain: long focus blocks, irregular sleep, and habits that slip during crunch
audiences.hook: you already live in tools — this one logs the day in a tap and syncs to a sheet you own

audiences.name: Remote Workers
audiences.pain: days blurring together with no commute to bookend them
audiences.hook: when every day is at the same desk, a daily log is what gives the week shape

audiences.name: Entrepreneurs
audiences.pain: wearing every hat and pushing health and habits to 'later'
audiences.hook: you track the business obsessively — this tracks the operator running it

audiences.name: People with ADHD
audiences.pain: starting systems that die by day three because they ask too much
audiences.hook: one screen, one tap, visible streaks — built to survive the day-three drop-off

audiences.name: Fitness Beginners
audiences.pain: not knowing which exercises to do or how to do them safely
audiences.hook: 42 exercises with animated form demos, so you start without a trainer

audiences.name: Busy Parents
audiences.pain: no time and no hands free for a fiddly app
audiences.hook: a full day logged in the time it takes to make one coffee

audiences.name: Freelancers
audiences.pain: unstructured days and no manager to create rhythm
audiences.hook: build your own rhythm when nobody else sets it for you

audiences.name: Medical Residents
audiences.pain: brutal shifts that wreck sleep and any routine
audiences.hook: track sleep and energy through rotations, offline, private on your device

audiences.name: Designers
audiences.pain: creative work that needs focus but rarely gets measured
audiences.hook: see your focus hours and mood trend against the work you actually shipped

audiences.name: Nurses
audiences.pain: 12-hour shifts, rotating schedules, and no time to log anything mid-round
audiences.hook: between rounds and charting, a tracker only survives if it takes one tap

audiences.name: Teachers
audiences.pain: days ruled by bells, grading at night, and their own routine coming last
audiences.hook: your day runs on someone else's schedule; your habits need to fit the gaps

audiences.name: Writers
audiences.pain: mood-dependent output, procrastination, and irregular creative rhythm
audiences.hook: creative work is moody; seeing the pattern helps you show up anyway

audiences.name: Athletes
audiences.pain: tracking training load but ignoring recovery, sleep, and mood
audiences.hook: your training is tracked; recovery, sleep and mood usually aren't

audiences.name: Consultants
audiences.pain: constant travel, client demands, and work-life lines that blur
audiences.hook: living out of airports, routine is the first thing to go

audiences.name: Night-Shift Workers
audiences.pain: disrupted sleep, off-hours living, and a real toll on health
audiences.hook: when your day is the world's night, sleep and energy need watching
